displayTitle: When the Source Won't Come Home
kicker: RAD SAFETY // SOURCE-OUT · SECURE · SURVEY · NOTIFY

beats.narration: Sooner or later you crank the source in and the meter refuses to drop. That's a source-out — the source never returned to shielding.

payload.title: WHEN THE SOURCE WON'T COME HOME
payload.sub: Secure it. Survey it. Notify — then hold the line.

beats.narration: Everything looks normal — that's the trap. Handle, turn count, the device, all can lie. After every exposure, survey the device and full guide tube.

payload.heading: Only the meter tells the truth

tiles.s: Bind means jam, free-spin means loose

beats.narration: First moves: stop cranking, never force a jam, back away. A bare hundred-curie iridium source reads forty-eight roentgen an hour at one meter. Distance cuts dose.

payload.heading: Distance does the heavy lifting
payload.note: 48 R/h AT 1 m — 6 MINUTES SPENDS THE YEAR

beats.narration: Then run the drill: survey the complete perimeter, set the rope by measured dose rate, keep continuous watch, account for every dosimeter, call the RSO now.

chips.s: Where dose rate says, not memory

beats.narration: Recovery is not your job. Retrieval belongs to trained, authorized teams with remote tools and a dose budget. Your three verbs — done fast, done exact.

payload.sub: Recovery is for trained, authorized hands — never yours.
